claim: Graffiti Conjecture 154 is FALSE under the standard-deviation reading of 'deviation' (the reading used by every known machine formalization), under BOTH standard conventions for 'average distance'.
statement_verbatim: 154. deviation of eigenvalues <= n / average distance.

statement_provenance.source: S. Fajtlowicz, 'Written on the Wall' (conjectures of Graffiti), July 2004 compilation
statement_provenance.extraction: PDF uses obfuscated Type-3 glyph names /XY (base-36: value=(digit(X)-10)*36+digit(Y)); decoded programmatically; '<=' rendered as glyph /AK. Decoded text in wow-decoded.txt.
statement_provenance.eigenvalue_convention: WoW preamble (decoded, verbatim): 'G always denotes a graph, n the number of its vertices and eigenvalues (unless it is explicitly stated) denote eigenvalues of the adjacency matrix of G.'
statement_provenance.average_distance_convention_evidence: WoW comment on conjecture 536 (decoded): 'The average distance of this graph is 3/2' for Paley graphs, which holds only under the distinct-pairs convention l = 2W/(n(n-1)). The Roucairol-Cazenave machine formalization instead averages all n^2 entries of the distance matrix (l = 2W/n^2). Both conventions are violated by the certificate instances lollipop(50,70) and lollipop(72,72).
statement_provenance.deviation_convention_evidence: WoW uses 'variance' (conj. 38, 143) and 'deviation' (conj. 39, 40, 140, 154, 244, 253) as distinct invariants, and says 'standard deviation' explicitly for degree sequences (conj. 27, 812, 813); deviation = sqrt(variance) = population standard deviation. Favaron-Maheo-Sacle 1993 (Discrete Math 111, the historical treatment of WoW 'deviation of eigenvalues' conjectures; Academia.edu mirror accessed 2026-06-11) defines mean distance over ordered distinct vertex pairs and defines the deviation of eigenvalues by a quadratic/square-root (RMS) formula, not mean absolute deviation. The only public machine formalization (Roucairol-Cazenave, refutationGBR/models/conjectures/GenerateGraph.rs, CONJECTURE==154 block) computes the population standard deviation of all adjacency eigenvalues (tools/calc.rs 'std' divides by len and takes sqrt). CAVEAT documented honestly: no surviving source defines 'deviation' next to conjecture 154 itself; if Graffiti's 'deviation' meant MEAN ABSOLUTE deviation, this family does NOT refute the conjecture; MAD non-violation at all three instances is PROVEN exactly in this bundle. The kill is claimed ONLY under the standard-deviation reading.

openness_evidence.wow_internal: WoW itself lists 154 among conjectures that PASSED the Brewster-Dinneen-Faber Los Alamos/U.Victoria computational tests (~200 conjectures tested, 40+ refuted, Aug 1990 - Aug 1991).
openness_evidence.modern: Roucairol-Cazenave, 'Refutation of Spectral Graph Theory Conjectures with Search Algorithms', arXiv:2409.18626 (Sep 2024), Table 1 row '154 O 50 any & tree - - - - - - - -': status Open, searched to size 50 with 8 search algorithms, all failed.
openness_evidence.fresh_search: Web search 2026-06-11 found no published refutation of Graffiti 154.

operational_inequality.lhs: population std dev of adjacency spectrum = sqrt(2m/n) EXACTLY (trace A = 0, trace A^2 = 2m); no floating point needed

counterexample_family: lollipop(t,p): complete graph K_t on vertices 0..t-1; path on vertices t..t+p-1; bridge edge (0,t). n=t+p, m=t(t-1)/2+p. Connected. W = C(t,2) + sum_{k=1..p}[k+(t-1)(k+1)] + C(p+1,3), independently re-verified by all-pairs BFS on the explicit edge list.

minimality_within_family.scan: exhaustive integer scan of all lollipop(t,p), 3<=t, 1<=p, n=t+p<=130
minimality_within_family.note: claim record said 'smallest found' - correct, though not unique at that order; (48,70) is one of four DP violators at n=118 and (50,70) one of two both-convention violators at n=120. Every N2 violator is a DP violator since n^7 > n^5(n-1)^2.

asymptotics: For t = p -> infinity: stddev = sqrt(2m/n) ~ sqrt(t/2) -> infinity while n/l stays bounded (~6); the violation ratio is unbounded, so the std-dev reading fails catastrophically, not marginally.
checker: check_graffiti154.py (this directory), clean-room, stdlib integers for the kill; numpy/mpmath/sympy only for spectral cross-checks and the exact MAD side-claim. Run log: checker_run.log.
